Week 2 · Build & Research

From flat pixels to navigable geometry.

A stereo image pair is run through a classical computer-vision / ML pipeline to produce a depth map, an unsupervised segmentation, an edge-derived AR line overlay, and a reconstructed 3D point cloud — rendered live, right here, in the browser.

Pipeline
Stereo → SGBM → 3D

01 / Live, in your browser

Try It — Run On Your Own Capture

Upload two photos taken a few centimetres apart for a real stereo reconstruction, a single photo for edges + segmentation, or a short panning video and two frames are pulled automatically. Everything below runs locally via OpenCV.js — nothing leaves this page.

02 / Reference input

Benchmark Pair

Pipeline validation input: a calibrated stereo pair from the Middlebury stereo benchmark set, used to confirm the pipeline against known-good depth data before running on live uploads above.

LEFT.JPG — camera A741×500
RIGHT.JPG — camera B741×500
Resolution
741×500
Baseline (approx.)
6 cm
Disparity range
0–128 px
Source
Stereo pair
03 / Learned & unsupervised structure

ML Visualizations

Two independent readings of the same scene: dense depth from stereo correspondence, and unsupervised region segmentation. Both feed the reconstruction stage below.

Depth heatmap — SGBM stereo matchingTURBO colormap
SLIC superpixel segmentation191 regions
  • 01Semi-Global Block Matching (SGBM) — computes per-pixel disparity by matching windows along epipolar lines between the two camera frames, penalizing discontinuities to keep surfaces coherent.
  • 02SLIC (Simple Linear Iterative Clustering) — an unsupervised clustering pass in a combined color + spatial space, grouping pixels into perceptually meaningful superpixels ahead of any labelling step.
04 / Overlay

AR Line Overlay

Structural edges extracted with Canny detection, then consolidated into line segments via a probabilistic Hough transform — the same category of geometry an AR layer would lock onto a live camera feed.

Canny edges + probabilistic Hough transform489 line segments
05 / Reconstruction

Capture-to-3D

The disparity map is reprojected into camera space with cv2.reprojectImageTo3D, colored from the source frame, and rendered as a live point cloud. Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom.

— points
Pipeline: OpenCV SGBM stereo matching · scikit-image SLIC segmentation · Canny + Hough line detection · reprojectImageTo3D point cloud · rendered with three.js. Swap LEFT.JPG / RIGHT.JPG for original captures to re-run on site footage.
